trajectory.description: Observation: RuntimeError - attention_mask (96000) doesn't match hidden state time dimension (299). The WavLM encoder outputs hidden states at a reduced temporal resolution (~320x downsampling), but the pooling code tries to multiply the waveform-level mask with the feature-level hidden states. Analysis: Need to compute feature-level attention mask using the encoder's _get_feature_vector_attention_mask method. Also need to fix the dataset/collate to create proper attention masks based on original lengths (not checking for zero values in waveform). Decision: Fix model.py attention mask handling, update dataset.py for proper mask creation, ensure train.py is complete, then run 5-epoch training.

Observation: Training completed 1 epoch (~5min) but crashed during validation with BFloat16 numpy conversion error. The logits are in bfloat16 from autocast, and .numpy() doesn't support bfloat16. Analysis: Need to cast to float32 before .numpy() in both train.py validation loop and predict.py. Also need to ensure train.py is complete (was truncated). Decision: Fix the bfloat16 conversion issue, complete train.py, and run 10 epochs to get a proper baseline.

trajectory.description: Observation: Training completed 10 epochs with best dev EER of 0.0035 on ASVspoof 2019 LA. Each epoch takes ~7.4 minutes. The key challenge is generalization to the 2021 LA eval set which has codec degradation (a-law, G.722, GSM, OPUS) and unseen attacks. Analysis: The dev EER is very low on clean data, but the test set has codec-degraded audio. The most impactful improvement would be adding codec simulation augmentation during training to improve robustness. I should also unfreeze more WavLM layers for better adaptation. Decision: Submit current best model for evaluation to get a baseline test score, then iterate with codec augmentation and other improvements.
